(sha=f2a5e9f5aa7f6c0b8f14e3e160a8516c85e30ad0)
commands.review_summary:
Adds a Codex/OpenAI `/login` device-code pairing command across Telegram native commands and shared chat command surfaces, plus OAuth failure-copy updates and a provider-auth SDK helper export.

PR surface: Source +492, Tests +395, Docs +1. Total +888 across 15 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the blocking PR defects: source inspection and the Mantis Telegram screenshot show `/login codex` posting the device code into a Telegram group, and current command-auth code shows channel allowFrom can feed senderIsOwner. I did not run tests because this was a read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Manual maintainer/security review is needed because the blockers involve credential trust boundaries, channel pairing policy, target-profile semantics, and plugin API compatibility.

target.title: Add Telegram /login Codex pairing flow
target.body:
## Summary

- Add `/login codex` for Codex/OpenAI device-code pairing from channel surfaces.
- Cover Telegram native commands plus the shared Web UI/dashboard, Discord, and Slack command dispatcher.
- Reuse the existing OpenAI Codex `device-code` auth flow and profile persistence path through a reusable non-TTY login helper.
- Update hard OAuth refresh failure copy to recommend `/login codex` in the current chat/channel while preserving the terminal fallback.

## What Problem This Solves

Codex/OpenAI OAuth expiry currently leaves channel-only or remote gateway operators with a terminal-only recovery path. When the user sends a normal message and the Codex token is expired, revoked, reused, or otherwise invalidated, the gateway now classifies that hard auth failure and replies with `/login codex` guidance instead of only a terminal command.

`/login codex` is explicit and owner/admin-gated. It sends the existing OpenAI Codex device-code URL/code into the invoking chat/channel so the owner can pair a fresh Codex login from a browser without exposing tokens or auto-starting auth in shared channels.

## Supported Surfaces

- Telegram: native `/login` command with chat/topic reply behavior.
- Web UI/dashboard: text slash command through `chat.send` and the shared reply dispatcher.
- Discord: native slash/text command through the shared command dispatcher.
- Slack: native slash/text command through the shared command dispatcher.

## Failure Flow

```mermaid
flowchart TD
  A["User sends normal request"] --> B["OpenClaw runs Codex/OpenAI model"]
  B --> C{"OAuth refresh succeeds?"}
  C -->|"yes"| D["Model reply continues normally"]
  C -->|"expired / revoked / token_invalidated / invalid_grant"| E["OAuth refresh failure classifier"]
  E --> F["Reply: send /login codex in this chat or channel"]
  B --> G{"Quota or rate limit?"}
  G -->|"yes"| H["Quota/rate-limit guidance, no reauth prompt"]
```

## Login Flow

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
  participant U as Owner/Admin
  participant C as Chat Channel
  participant O as OpenClaw
  participant A as OpenAI Device Auth
  U->>C: /login codex
  C->>O: authorized command turn
  O->>C: verification URL and user code
  U->>A: pair in browser
  A-->>O: provider auth result
  O->>O: persist OpenAI auth profile and clear lockouts
  O->>C: Codex login complete
```

## Security / UX Notes

- `/login codex`, `/login openai`, and `/login openai-codex` normalize to provider `openai` and method `device-code`.
- Starting login is owner/admin-gated; authorized non-owners are rejected.
- Active login flows are deduped per channel/chat/thread/agent/provider.
- The channel wrappers send only the provider’s device verification URL/code message and sanitized status text; they do not send or log OAuth tokens.
- The flow stays explicit and user-triggered rather than auto-starting OAuth after a model failure.
- The reusable login helper is exported through the focused non-deprecated `openclaw/plugin-sdk/provider-auth-runtime` subpath, not the broad `agent-runtime` barrel.

## Validation

- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/commands-login.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts src/agents/auth-profiles/oauth-refresh-failure.test.ts`
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/agents/auth-profiles/oauth-refresh-failure.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-native-commands.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.test.src.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/test-src.tsbuildinfo`
- `node scripts/check-extension-plugin-sdk-boundary.mjs --mode=plugin-sdk-internal`
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 <changed files>`
- `git diff --check`

Additional review follow-up:

- Moved `runModelsAuthLoginFlow` off `openclaw/plugin-sdk/agent-runtime` and onto `openclaw/plugin-sdk/provider-auth-runtime`.
- Added `/login` to slash-command docs after CI caught the new built-in alias.
- Updated the Control UI slash-command browser-import snapshot after CI caught the shared registry import-order change.
- Re-ran `check-deprecated-api-usage`, focused Vitest, `tsgo` test-src typecheck, formatter, and plugin SDK boundary validation after the SDK-boundary/docs change.

## Evidence

- Added shared command tests for `/login codex` on Web UI, Discord, and Slack command surfaces.
- Added Telegram tests for `/login codex` sending the device-code message before completion, owner-only rejection, and active-flow dedupe.
- Added OAuth classifier coverage for `token_invalidated`.
- Added failure-copy coverage showing hard OpenAI/Codex auth failures now recommend `/login codex` in the current chat/channel.
- Quota and rate-limit failures continue to use quota/rate-limit guidance rather than reauth copy.
- Local focused validation passed for the changed auth, auto-reply, shared command, and Telegram command test files.

## Notes

- Extension-wide test typecheck still reports existing QA-lab crabline type errors unrelated to this PR; filtered output shows no Telegram `/login` type errors.

Refs #97984
